description_long: This is a traditional axe featuring a long, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural brown finish and visible wood grain. The head of the axe is made of dark, weathered metal, with a single broad cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side. The blade is wedge-shaped, tapering to a sharp edge, and the mounting appears robust, with the metal head securely fitted onto the handle. The handle is cylindrical and tapers slightly towards the end, providing a comfortable grip for swinging. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, giving the axe a utilitarian, functional appearance. The overall design suggests it is intended for chopping wood or similar tasks in an urban or suburban environment.
description: A traditional axe with a long wooden handle and a dark metal head, featuring a single broad cutting edge and a flat poll. The design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for chopping tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the wedge-shaped metal blade with a clear cutting edge.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the circular cross-section of the handle and the secure mounting of the metal head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the straight alignment of the handle and the back of the metal head.
attribute_handle_type: Long, slightly curved cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth finish and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a sharp edge and a flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
